descriptor: Vertically integrated bauxite-alumina-aluminum producer. HQ Pittsburgh, PA. NYSE: AA.
snapshot: US, Canada, Australia, Brazil, Spain, Iceland

assets.note: ~27 locations per company site. Key production assets below; refinery system ~17 Mt/yr installed across 6 refineries (AU/BR/ES).

sites.name: WA alumina refineries (Pinjarra, Wagerup; Kwinana curtailed 2024)
Warrick Operations
sites.type: Smelter + rolling, duty-paid MW premium capture

sites.name: Wagerup gallium facility (FID with AU/US/JP govts)

suppliers: Internal bauxite chain (Huntly, Willowdale, Juruti, now Worsley): largely self-supplied feedstock
Energy: long-term power contracts per smelter region (Québec hydro, Iberian renewables); terms not disclosed
**South32**: seller of Worsley bauxite/alumina assets to Alcoa, closed Q2'26
jvs: **CITIC + Marubeni** (likely): minority partners in Portland, VIC smelter JV
**Ignitis/Spanish partners**: San Ciprián renewables arrangements around restart (likely)
**Australia-US-Japan governments**: co-investment in Wagerup gallium facility, FID 2026
customers: ~35% of 2026 primary shipments priced on duty-paid Midwest premium, ~25% duty-unpaid (company guidance)
US billet/slab/foundry buyers: names not disclosed, sold on LME + regional premium + product premium
Canadian smelter output flows to US customers, paying 50% Section 232 on full invoice value
Rolled products (Warrick): North American can sheet buyers, names not disclosed

angles: Record Midwest premium (~$2,529/t, May 2026): Alcoa's US smelters capture it fully, Canadian units pay tariff first
~$35M sequential added 232 cost on Canadian metal in Q2: origin arbitrage between AA's US vs Canada units is live
Restarts (San Ciprián, Portland, Alumar) add supply while premium is at records: watch P1020 spot offers loosening
EPS missed on alumina softness: refinery margin squeezed while smelter margin fat, spread trade between API and MWP
Worsley acquisition lengthens alumina position: they get longer alumina just as index weakens, watch resale flows
Gallium FID with 3 governments: strategic-metals credibility play, opens critical-minerals conversations

developments.event: Q2 results: record $4.0B revenue, adj EBITDA $901M, EPS miss ($2.12 vs $2.31 est)
Closed acquisition of South32's Worsley alumina assets, Western Australia
Portland smelter restart complete, ~95% of capacity
Gallium facility FID at Wagerup with Australian, US, Japanese governments
San Ciprián smelter restart completed Apr 7
US Midwest premium hit record ~$2,182/t on tariff tension, kept climbing

tariffs: Section 232 aluminum at 50% on full invoice value of Canadian-origin metal; no Canada exemption since Mar 2025
Tariff cost on Canadian imports rose ~$35M sequentially into Q2'26 (company guidance)
Midwest premium ~$2,529/t (May 2026), >40% of all-in US aluminum cost: tariff-driven, record level
US smelting assets (Warrick, Massena) are net beneficiaries; Canadian assets net payers
Washington aluminum incentive program under discussion: watch for smelter-restart subsidies changing US supply
financials: Q2'26: revenue $4.0B (record, +24% seq), adj net income $562M, adj EBITDA $901M
EPS $2.12 missed $2.31 estimate: alumina price softness the drag
Aluminum output 636kt, +5% seq on San Ciprián completion
Guidance supported by record Midwest premium as profit driver
Watch: alumina headwinds vs smelter strength, segment mix shifting
market: US Midwest premium: ~$2,529/t (early May 2026), record; was 98.5-99¢/lb per CRU earlier in the ramp
US all-in aluminum topped $6,000/t (2026): tariff cost floor discussion now standard
LME aluminum: strength cited in Q2 results; premium doing more work than LME flat price
Alumina index: softening, hitting refinery margins (Q2 EPS miss driver)
Global flows rerouting around US tariff wall per Alcoa CEO (Fastmarkets)
